name: Sviata Vechera -- Christmas Eve
recipeInstructions:
1. The family fasts all day before Christmas Eve.
2. The house is put in order, but no outside work is done.
3. All members wear festive clothing for the celebration.
4. Prepare a table with hay spread out, then covered with cloth.
5. Place a large kolach flanked by candles at the center of the table.
6. Set a dish of salt and honey at the head of the household.
7. On a sideboard, place a lit candle with a dish of kutia for the deceased.
8. Prepare an extra place setting for an unexpected guest.
9. Place a sheaf of wheat in a corner to symbolize abundance.
10. Begin the meal when the first star is sighted by children.
11. The male head of household serves kutia and invites spirits of ancestors to join.
12. Invite forces of nature to partake, protecting the family from calamities.
13. Perform regional rituals, such as sharing bread with honey.
14. The female head of household greets everyone "Christ is born" and responds "Let us glorify Him."
15. Serve dishes in traditional order: kutia or uzvar at the end.
16. After the meal, visit barn animals and add kutia to their food.
17. On Christmas Day and subsequent days, share treats with neighbors.
18. Sing carols throughout the Christmas season.
19. On New Year's Eve (January 13), prepare regional foods and welcome the new year with zasivannia.
20. On Christmas Day (January 19), serve a smaller kutia meal called holodna kutia.
21. After the church service, go to a local stream or river for blessing of waters.
Note: This recipe is a modern adaptation and may include ingredients not traditionally used in the original Ukrainian celebration.
